text.title: NoSQL Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the NoSQL Patterns methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Spec MUST start from a documented access-pattern register (primary queries, QPS, latency budget) before declaring entities or collections.

rationale.source: MongoDB schema-design guide; Cassandra data-modelling rules

rule.rationale: Entity-first NoSQL designs miss the access patterns that drive partition-key choice and embed-vs-reference verdicts; reshaping later requires a full table copy.

rule.statement: Embed child documents only when the child set is bounded (1:few, < ~100 items); reference for 1:many and many:many.

rationale.source: MongoDB 16MB document limit; index-rebuild perf studies

rule.rationale: Unbounded embedded arrays grow the document past the limit, slow updates, and bloat indexes; references keep documents small and updates atomic.

rule.statement: Every Redis prefix MUST carry an explicit TTL policy (`ttl_seconds`); raw keys without TTL or eviction policy are forbidden.

rationale.source: Redis docs - eviction; production incident retros

rule.rationale: Keys without TTL accumulate until maxmemory; Redis then evicts randomly or rejects writes, breaking unrelated callers.

rule.statement: All Redis keys MUST follow `{service}:{entity}:{id}` namespace; raw keys (e.g. `user:123`) are rejected.

rationale.source: Redis best practice; multi-service collision postmortems

rule.rationale: Services sharing a Redis instance collide on identical key names; namespacing eliminates cross-service corruption.

rule.statement: For Cassandra / DynamoDB, the partition key MUST be chosen to match the primary access query and documented as immutable; time-series uses `(entity_id, time_bucket)` to avoid hot partitions.

rationale.source: Cassandra data-modelling rules; DynamoDB hot-partition postmortems

rule.rationale: Partition keys cannot be changed without a full table copy; a wrong choice locks the team out of scaling.

rule.statement: Production MongoDB collections MUST enable `$jsonSchema` validation; un-validated collections rot and accumulate incompatible documents.
rule.rationale: Loose schemas catch bad writes only after they corrupt downstream queries; write-time validation is cheap and prevents silent drift.

rule.statement: Every MongoDB / Neo4j field used in a query predicate MUST be indexed; missing indexes are flagged as critical in the spec.

rationale.source: MongoDB explain() benchmarks; Neo4j MATCH-without-index incidents

rule.rationale: Unindexed predicates degrade from milliseconds to minutes at modest scale; the spec must enumerate the index list per collection / label.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
